tools.name: search
tools.description: Search SAGE-connected stores and SAGE's verified public independent-retailer inventory network for a specific rare, vintage, discontinued, one-of-one, local, secondhand, or hard-to-find general-audience physical product. Results require fresh positive source evidence, a crawlable SAGE item page, and

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Fetch one exact result id returned by this public-network search. SAGE rechecks the indexed retailer evidence before returning its source, URL, price, freshness, availability evidence, and limits. Read-only; never use an id not returned by search.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "search" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Search SAGE Infinite Search when ordinary product search is unlikely to find a rare, vintage, discontinued, one-of-one, local, secondhand, or poorly identified item. Returns only public independent-retailer candidates with fresh positive source evidence. When results is empty, you must show recovery" → after: "Search SAGE-connected stores and SAGE's verified public independent-retailer inventory network for a specific rare, vintage, discontinued, one-of-one, local, secondhand, or hard-to-find general-audience physical product. Results require fresh positive source evidence, a crawlable SAGE item page, and"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Fetch one exact SAGE result by the opaque id returned from search. Rechecks the indexed source and returns its retailer, URL, price, availability evidence, freshness, and safety limits. Read-only; never use an id that did not come from search." → after: "Fetch one exact result id returned by this public-network search. SAGE rechecks the indexed retailer evidence before returning its source, URL, price, freshness, availability evidence, and limits. Read-only; never use an id not returned by search."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool removed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "search_local_inventory" is no longer advertised. Removal can precede a shadowing replacement from another server.
